reasoning: Current market search firmly establishes Bitcoin's price around $70,000 as of February 14-15, 2026. The resolution criteria requires the price to hit $120,000 by the end of February (in 13 days). This would require an approx. 71% price surge over just 13 days. Given Bitcoin's massive market capitalization, a sudden 71% jump in such a short window is exceedingly rare and currently lacks any foreseeable realistic catalyst. Furthermore, the Polymarket implied probability was already at just 0.01 on February 9. Therefore, based on the very short timeframe, large distance to the price target, and crowd consensus, the true probability is likely under 1%, leading me to select the minimum lower bound of 0.02.
